description_long: This object is a miniature pagoda structure set on a rounded base, featuring a multi-tiered roof with distinct upward-curving eaves. The main tower rises above a lower platform, with the roof tiers decreasing in size as they ascend, culminating in a decorative finial at the apex. The base includes additional architectural elements, such as small adjoining buildings or walls, suggesting a temple complex. The surfaces are rendered in muted tones of gray, brown, and hints of red, evoking traditional stone and tile materials. The overall form is compact, with intricate roof detailing and a clear separation between stories. The structure is reminiscent of East Asian religious architecture, likely intended as a decorative or symbolic element in an urban park or garden setting.
description: A compact, multi-tiered pagoda with upward-curving eaves, ornate roof detailing, and a decorative finial, set on a rounded base with adjoining structures.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main pagoda tower with its tiered roofs and finial, as well as the adjoining lower structures on the left.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the side of the pagoda, revealing more of the adjoining buildings and the layered roof structure.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back of the pagoda and the rounded base, with the roof tiers and finial still visible but less prominent.
description_view_3: This view provides a diagonal perspective, showing both the main pagoda and the attached structures, with clear visibility of the roof tiers and base.
